page: Microsoft Azure E32-16s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv4 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The configuration includes 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data positions the E32-16s_v4 in the average performance tier across most compute and memory operations. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is recorded at 1460.59, while multi-core performance reaches 14909.58. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block speed of 831.89 GB/sec and a large block read speed of 85.2 GB/sec. In database-oriented workloads, the server achieves 14.09 million operations per second in Redis SET operations and a PassMark database operations score of 5801.91. For language model tasks, the server processes 791.15 tokens per second for prompt processing on a small 135M model, though performance drops to a weak 24.28 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E32-16s_v4 offers a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable choice for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU processing. The lack of bundled local storage means storage costs are externalized to attached Premium Storage volumes. This server is best suited for memory-intensive applications such as relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data buffering, and moderate enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E32-16s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a substantial 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks place the server in the average tier, with a PassMark CPU score of 15961.04 and a Redis SET throughput of 14.09 million operations per second. This configuration is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy workloads that require a moderate CPU footprint. It is highly suited for database operations, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for database operations and in-memory caching.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and dedicated Intel Xeon performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory providing a high 16.0 GB per core ratio.
